title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 69.
content:
There was an Old Man of Coblenz,
    The length of whose legs was immense;
    He went with one prance,
    From Turkey to France,
    That surprising Old Man of Coblenz.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 69.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
